 {"id":"bd-1cwg","title":"Epic: True End-to-End Integration Tests (No Mocks)","description":"# Epic: True End-to-End Integration Tests (No Mocks)

## Executive Summary

This epic establishes a comprehensive suite of **100% realistic end-to-end integration tests** for RCH (Remote Compilation Helper). Unlike the existing mock-based test suite, these tests will:

- Connect to **real remote workers** via actual SSH
- Execute **real cargo/compiler commands** on those workers
- Transfer **real project files** via rsync
- Retrieve **real build artifacts** back to the local machine
- Validate **output correctness** against local execution

## Merged Scope: Fleet & Worker Lifecycle Tests (formerly bd-guef)

This epic now also includes the fleet and worker lifecycle E2E tests that were previously in a separate epic. This consolidation reduces duplication and provides a single canonical, comprehensive E2E suite.

### Fleet & Worker Lifecycle Coverage

1. **Multi-Worker Scenarios (bd-2s8m)**: Load balancing, priority selection, cached project locality, fleet scaling, heterogeneous workers
2. **Worker Lifecycle**: Registration, health monitoring, removal, updates, disconnects
3. **Test Logging Standardization (bd-2zsu)**: Structured logging across all test files

## Background & Motivation

### Current Test Gap Analysis

The existing RCH test suite provides good coverage for:
- Command classification logic (e2e_hook.rs)
- Daemon lifecycle and API (e2e_daemon.rs)
- Worker connectivity patterns (e2e_worker.rs)
- Fleet operations (e2e_fleet.rs)

However, all tests use **mock infrastructure**:
- `RCH_MOCK_SSH=1` environment variable bypasses real SSH
- `WorkersFixture::mock_local()` creates fake workers
- No actual compilation happens on remote machines
- No real artifact transfer is validated

### Why This Matters

RCH is a **transparent compilation offloading system** for AI coding agents. The agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) have no awareness that their `cargo build` or `cargo test` commands execute remotely. This transparency requires **absolute correctness** in:

1. **Project synchronization** - Every file the build needs must arrive
2. **Command execution** - Exact same behavior as local execution
3. **Output capture** - Stdout/stderr must be byte-for-byte identical
4. **Exit codes** - Test failures (101), build errors (1) must propagate
5. **Artifact retrieval** - Compiled binaries must work locally

A bug in any of these areas breaks the illusion of local execution and causes agent confusion or failures.

## Test Categories

### Cargo/Rust Compilation (bd-12hi)
- cargo build, test, check, clippy, bench
- All CompilationKind variants

### SSH Infrastructure (bd-255k)
- Connection, authentication, command execution
- Output capture, timeout handling

### Project Sync (bd-1f2v)
- rsync transfer, excludes, incremental sync

### Artifact Transfer (bd-20zz)
- Binary retrieval, correctness verification

### Error Handling & Recovery (bd-23n3)
- Network failures, worker crashes, fail-open behavior
- Circuit breaker state transitions

### C/C++ Compilers (bd-v9pq)
- gcc, g++, clang, make, cmake, ninja

### Output Validation (bd-2ga8)
- Color preservation, ANSI codes, terminal semantics

### Performance & Timing (bd-1nhd)
- Hook classification <1ms, full pipeline <15% overhead

### Multi-Worker Scenarios (bd-2s8m)
- Load balancing, priority selection, heterogeneous workers
- Worker registration, health monitoring, lifecycle

### Test Logging Standardization (bd-2zsu)
- Structured JSON logging across all test files

## Success Criteria

- All CompilationKind variants tested with real workers
- Zero tolerance for output differences from local execution
- Exit codes must match exactly
- Tests pass reliably in CI with real infrastructure
- All tests produce structured, parseable logs
- Worker lifecycle scenarios pass in CI
- Multi-worker load balancing verified
- 100% structured logging compliance","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:10:09.472523589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:27:10.595954929Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-2s8m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-2zsu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1djk","title":"Unit Tests for RchConsole Wrapper","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Tests for RchConsole

The RchConsole wrapper is the #1 bottleneck in the dependency graph (blocks 11+ tasks). It must be thoroughly tested.

### Test Categories

#### 1. Context Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_new_detects_context() {
    // This is environment-dependent, so test with explicit context
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Interactive);
    assert!(console.is_rich());
    assert!(console.is_colored());
    assert!(!console.is_machine());
}

#[test]
fn test_machine_context_properties() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    assert!(!console.is_rich());
    assert!(!console.is_colored());
    assert!(console.is_machine());
}

#[test]
fn test_plain_context_properties() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Plain);
    assert!(!console.is_rich());
    assert!(!console.is_colored());
    assert!(!console.is_machine());
}
```

#### 2. Output Suppression Tests
```rust
use std::io::Cursor;

#[test]
fn test_print_rich_suppressed_in_machine_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    // This should do nothing, not panic
    console.print_rich("[bold]test[/]");
}

#[test]
fn test_print_plain_suppressed_in_machine_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    // This should do nothing
    console.print_plain("test");
}

#[test]
fn test_print_error_always_outputs() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    // Errors should still output (to stderr)
    console.print_error("Test Error", "This is a test");
    // Note: Can't easily capture stderr in unit test, but should not panic
}
```

#### 3. Fallback Method Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_print_or_plain_uses_rich_in_interactive() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Interactive);
    // Should use rich path (not panic)
    console.print_or_plain("[bold]rich[/]", "plain");
}

#[test]
fn test_print_or_plain_uses_plain_in_plain_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Plain);
    console.print_or_plain("[bold]rich[/]", "plain");
}

#[test]
fn test_rule_fallback_in_plain_mode() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Plain);
    console.rule(Some("Title"));
    console.rule(None);
}
```

#### 4. JSON Output Tests
```rust
#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
struct TestData {
    key: String,
    value: i32,
}

#[test]
fn test_print_json_serializes_correctly() {
    let console = RchConsole::with_context(OutputContext::Machine);
    let data = TestData { key: "test".to_string(), value: 42 };
    // Should not panic
    let result = console.print_json(&data);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

#### 5. Width Detection Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_width_returns_reasonable_value() {
    let console = RchConsole::new();
    let width = console.width();
    // Should be at least 40 and at most 500 (reasonable terminal bounds)
    assert!(width >= 40);
    assert!(width <= 500);
}
```

#### 6. Thread Safety Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_global_console_is_thread_safe() {
    use std::thread;
    
    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10)
        .map(|_| {
            thread::spawn(|| {
                // Access global console from multiple threads
                let _ = CONSOLE.width();
                CONSOLE.print_plain("test");
            })
        })
        .collect();
    
    for handle in handles {
        handle.join().unwrap();
    }
}
```

### Test Coverage Requirements

| Method | Test Coverage |
|--------|--------------|
| new() | Context detection |
| with_context() | All 5 contexts |
| is_rich() | All contexts |
| is_colored() | All contexts |
| is_machine() | All contexts |
| width() | Returns valid value |
| print_rich() | Suppression in non-rich |
| print_plain() | Suppression in machine |
| print_or_plain() | Both paths |
| print_error() | Always outputs |
| print_success/warning/info() | Rich and plain fallbacks |
| print_json() | Serialization |
| rule() | Rich and plain fallbacks |
| CONSOLE static | Thread safety |

 {"id":"bd-2ga8","title":"True E2E Output Validation & Correctness Tests","description":"# Feature: True E2E Output Validation & Correctness Tests

## Purpose

Test that output from remote compilation (stdout, stderr, exit codes, terminal colors) is correctly preserved and propagated to the calling agent.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

Output validation tests MUST use structured JSON logging:

### Output-Specific Log Events

```json
// Output capture start
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_101","phase":"execute","msg":"Command executed","data":{"cmd":"cargo test","mode":"remote","worker":"css"}}

// Exit code verification
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_101","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code check","data":{"expected":101,"actual":101,"match":true,"semantic":"tests_failed"}}

// stdout/stderr comparison
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_stderr_preservation","phase":"verify","msg":"Output comparison","data":{"stream":"stderr","local_bytes":1234,"remote_bytes":1234,"match":true,"hash_local":"abc123","hash_remote":"abc123"}}

// Color code detection
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_ansi_colors","phase":"verify","msg":"ANSI codes detected","data":{"total_codes":15,"colors":["red","green","yellow"],"styles":["bold","underline"]}}

// Streaming latency
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_streaming","phase":"timing","msg":"First byte latency","data":{"local_first_byte_ms":50,"remote_first_byte_ms":85,"overhead_ms":35}}

// Mismatch details (on failure)
{"ts":"...","level":"ERROR","test":"test_output_mismatch","phase":"verify","msg":"Output mismatch detected","data":{"stream":"stdout","line":42,"local":"expected text","remote":"actual text","diff":"<unified diff snippet>"}}
```

### Metrics to Log

1. **Exit codes**: expected, actual, semantic meaning
2. **Output size**: bytes per stream, truncation if any
3. **Content hash**: SHA-256 of local vs remote
4. **ANSI codes**: detected codes, colors, styles
5. **Timing**: first byte latency, total duration
6. **Diff details**: on mismatch, show context

## Why This Matters

- AI agents parse compilation output to understand errors and fix code
- Corrupted or missing output makes agents unable to diagnose issues
- Terminal colors help agents distinguish warnings from errors
- Exit codes determine whether agents retry or report failure

## Output Types to Verify

### Exit Code Semantics

1. **Exit 0** = success (all tests passed, build succeeded)
   - Log: semantic="success", verify stdout contains success indicators
   
2. **Exit 1** = compilation/build error
   - Log: semantic="build_error", verify error message in stderr
   
3. **Exit 101** = cargo test: tests ran but some failed
   - Log: semantic="tests_failed", count failed tests in output
   
4. **Exit 128+N** = killed by signal N
   - Log: signal number, signal name
   
5. **All non-zero** denies local re-execution
   - Log: verify no local fallback on compilation error

### stdout/stderr Preservation

1. **Compiler error messages** (line numbers, context)
   - Log: line number parsed correctly, context preserved
   
2. **Test output** (pass/fail indicators, assertion messages)
   - Log: test count, pass/fail breakdown
   
3. **Warning messages** (not lost or mixed)
   - Log: warning count, no interleaving corruption
   
4. **Progress indicators** (cargo build percentage)
   - Log: progress updates received
   
5. **Multi-line output** (stack traces, long errors)
   - Log: line count, no truncation

### Terminal Formatting

1. **ANSI color codes preserved** (TERM, NO_COLOR, CARGO_TERM_COLOR)
   - Log: TERM value, color codes detected, preservation status
   
2. **Bold/underline for emphasis**
   - Log: style codes found
   
3. **Color-coded test results** (green pass, red fail)
   - Log: pass=green, fail=red verification
   
4. **cargo's progress bar and spinner**
   - Log: carriage returns, spinner frames
   
5. **Wide characters and unicode**
   - Log: unicode points found, correct rendering

### Streaming Behavior

1. **Output streams in real-time** (not batched at end)
   - Log: timestamp of first byte, timestamp of last byte
   
2. **stderr and stdout interleaved correctly**
   - Log: interleaving order preserved
   
3. **Large output (>1MB) handled** without truncation
   - Log: total bytes, no truncation marker
   
4. **Binary data in output** doesn't corrupt stream
   - Log: binary bytes detected, stream integrity

## Merged E2E Scenarios (Hook/Feature-Flag/Error Display)

### Hook Protocol Integrity (from bd-3obh)

1. **Hook JSON response remains pristine**
   - Run: `echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}' | rch hook --stdin`
   - Verify stdout is valid JSON (no ANSI), stderr only for rich output
   - Log: json_valid=true, ansi_present=false, stderr_bytes

2. **Compilation output passthrough**
   - Run: `rch compile cargo build` on a fixture with known output
   - Verify stdout/stderr identical to local execution (byte-for-byte after normalization)
   - Log: stdout/stderr hashes, diff on mismatch

### Rich Output Gating & Feature Flags (from bd-dej3)

1. **Compile-time rich-ui off**
   - Build without rich-ui feature flag
   - Verify no rich formatting emitted anywhere
   - Log: build_flags, ansi_present=false

2. **Runtime gating**
   - With rich-ui on, ensure `--json` output is still plain JSON and stderr-only rich output
   - With `NO_COLOR=1`, ensure ANSI stripped
   - Log: TERM/NO_COLOR values and ansi detection

### Error Display Verification (from bd-vp67)

1. **SSH connection refused display**
   - Expected: error code, host/port, remediation hint
   - Verify NO_COLOR removes ANSI

2. **Config parse error display**
   - Show file path, line number, and snippet context

3. **Build failure display**
   - Compiler error text preserved verbatim

4. **JSON error mode**
   - `--json` outputs structured error to stdout
   - Error object includes code + message + remediation (if available)

5. **Severity differentiation**
   - Warning vs error output visually distinct (icons/text), no crash

6. **stderr-only guarantee**
   - Non-JSON errors never pollute stdout (stdout empty or valid JSON)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Exit codes match local execution
- [ ] stdout/stderr byte-for-byte identical (after normalization)
- [ ] Colors preserved when TERM is set
- [ ] Streaming works for long builds
- [ ] No output corruption for any test case
- [ ] Hook JSON responses are valid and ANSI-free
- [ ] Rich output never contaminates stdout in --json mode
- [ ] Error displays include code + remediation and respect NO_COLOR
- [ ] ALL tests emit structured JSON logs
- [ ] Mismatches logged with detailed diffs

## Technical Notes

- Use std::process::Output for capture
- Test with various TERM values (xterm-256color, dumb, etc)
- Verify against local execution baseline
- Log raw byte sequences for debugging

## Dependencies

- Requires bd-3saj (test infrastructure)
- Requires bd-12hi (cargo compilation produces output to verify)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:13.741339923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T07:21:59.688655882Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T07:19:35.572737775Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","depends_on_id":"bd-12hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","depends_on_id":"bd-1cwg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-2ga8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"StormyAnchor: Created E2E output validation tests (scripts/e2e_bd-2ga8.sh). 13 tests covering exit codes, stdout/stderr, hook JSON, ANSI colors, large output, error display.  {"id":"bd-2juk","title":"Doctor --fix Auto-Starts Daemon","description":"# Doctor --fix Auto-Starts Daemon

## Problem Statement
When running `rch doctor`, the daemon status is checked and reported:
```
Daemon
  ⚠ Daemon is not running
    Socket not found: /run/user/1000/rch.sock
    Suggestion: Start with: rch daemon start
```

But `rch doctor --fix` doesn't actually start the daemon. Users have to manually run `rch daemon start` separately.

## Solution: Doctor --fix Starts Daemon if Not Running
When --fix is passed and daemon is not running, start it automatically.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add daemon start logic to check_daemon function
In `rch/src/doctor.rs`, find the daemon check section and add fix logic:

```rust
async fn check_daemon(
    checks: &mut Vec<CheckResult>,
    ctx: &OutputContext,
    options: &DoctorOptions,
) {
    let style = ctx.theme();
    let socket_path = Path::new(crate::daemon::DEFAULT_SOCKET_PATH);

    if !ctx.is_json() {
        println!("{}", style.highlight("Daemon"));
        println!();
    }

    // Track fix info
    let mut fix_applied = false;
    let mut fix_message = String::new();

    let mut result = if socket_path.exists() {
        // Existing check logic...
        CheckResult {
            category: "daemon".to_string(),
            name: "daemon_running".to_string(),
            status: CheckStatus::Pass,
            message: "Daemon is running".to_string(),
            details: Some(socket_path.display().to_string()),
            suggestion: None,
            fixable: false,
            fix_applied: false,
            fix_message: None,
        }
    } else {
        CheckResult {
            category: "daemon".to_string(),
            name: "daemon_running".to_string(),
            status: CheckStatus::Warning,
            message: "Daemon is not running".to_string(),
            details: Some(format!("Socket not found: {}", socket_path.display())),
            suggestion: Some("Start with: rch daemon start".to_string()),
            fixable: true,  // Mark as fixable!
            fix_applied: false,
            fix_message: None,
        }
    };

    // Try to fix if requested
    if options.fix && result.fixable && result.status != CheckStatus::Pass {
        if options.dry_run {
            fix_message = "Would start RCH daemon".to_string();
            if !ctx.is_json() {
                println!(
                    "  {} {}",
                    StatusIndicator::DryRun.display(style),
                    &fix_message
                );
            }
            result.fix_message = Some(fix_message);
        } else {
            match start_daemon_for_doctor().await {
                Ok(()) => {
                    fix_applied = true;
                    fix_message = "Started RCH daemon".to_string();
                    if !ctx.is_json() {
                        println!(
                            "  {} {}",
                            StatusIndicator::Fixed.display(style),
                            &fix_message
                        );
                    }
                    result.status = CheckStatus::Pass;
                    result.message = "Daemon started (fixed)".to_string();
                    result.details = Some(socket_path.display().to_string());
                    result.fixable = false;
                    result.fix_applied = true;
                    result.fix_message = Some(fix_message);
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    fix_message = format!("Failed to start daemon: {}", e);
                    if !ctx.is_json() {
                        println!(
                            "  {} {}",
                            StatusIndicator::Error.display(style),
                            &fix_message
                        );
                    }
                    result.fix_message = Some(fix_message);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    print_check_result(&result, ctx);
    checks.push(result);
}
```

### 2. Create daemon start helper function
```rust
async fn start_daemon_for_doctor() -> Result<()> {
    use tokio::process::Command;
    use std::process::Stdio;
    
    let rchd_path = which_rchd()
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("rchd not found in PATH"))?;
    
    debug!("Starting daemon: {}", rchd_path.display());
    
    let mut cmd = Command::new("nohup");
    cmd.arg(&rchd_path)
        .stdout(Stdio::null())
        .stderr(Stdio::null())
        .stdin(Stdio::null())
        .kill_on_drop(false);
    
    cmd.spawn()?;
    
    // Wait for socket to appear
    let socket_path = Path::new(crate::daemon::DEFAULT_SOCKET_PATH);
    for i in 0..30 {
        tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
        if socket_path.exists() {
            debug!("Socket appeared after {}ms", (i + 1) * 100);
            return Ok(());
        }
    }
    
    Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Daemon started but socket not found after 3s"))
}
```

### 3. Ensure fix order: hooks before daemon
In the main doctor flow, ensure hooks are fixed before daemon is started. This matters because:
- Daemon auto-installs hooks on startup (bd-1emo)
- If hooks are already installed by doctor --fix (bd-3pam), daemon sees them as already installed
- This ensures idempotency

The execution order in doctor should be:
1. System checks
2. SSH checks (with --fix for permissions)
3. **Hook checks (with --fix to install)** ← First
4. **Daemon checks (with --fix to start)** ← Second
5. Worker checks (only if daemon is running)

### 4. JSON Output Format
When running `rch doctor --fix --json`:

    {
      "category": "daemon",
      "name": "daemon_running",
      "status": "pass",
      "message": "Daemon started (fixed)",
      "details": "/run/user/1000/rch.sock",
      "suggestion": null,
      "fixable": false,
      "fix_applied": true,
      "fix_message": "Started RCH daemon"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total": 10,
    "passed": 10,
    "warnings": 0,
    "failed": 0,
    "fixed": 2
  }
}
```

When running `rch doctor --fix --dry-run --json`:

    "total": 10,

    "failed": 0,
    "fixed": 0,
    "would_fix": 2
  }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-2m7j","title":"Idea: Enforce min_local_time_ms + speedup gating","description":"## Background
RCH advertises min_local_time_ms and remote_speedup_threshold but the hook currently does not gate offloading with these signals. This risks offloading tiny builds where overhead dominates, creating a mismatch between documented behavior and real behavior.

## Goals
- Honor min_local_time_ms and remote_speedup_threshold at hook decision time.
- Use real timing history + worker speed scores to estimate local time and speedup.
- Preserve fail-open behavior and sub-5ms classification budget.

## Non-Goals
- Changing classification rules or supported commands.
- Introducing new telemetry backends.

## Design / Approach
1) Extend build history data model to store remote duration + estimated local for each intercepted command (per project + kind).
2) Hook computes: predicted_local_ms (from history + worker speed score) and predicted_speedup.
3) Skip remote if predicted_local_ms < min_local_time_ms OR predicted_speedup < remote_speedup_threshold.
4) Surface the decision in verbose output + JSON diagnostics.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add timing fields to command result pipeline (hook -> daemon selection -> hook).
- Record per-command timing metrics in a lightweight local history store (ring buffer).
- Add estimation logic: fallback order = history median -> worker speed score -> conservative default.
- Integrate gating in hook after classification and before worker selection.
- Update docs/config help to align behavior.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation math (min_local_time_ms + speedup threshold edge cases).
- Unit: history fallback order.
- Integration: hook skips remote when predicted local < threshold.
- Integration: hook still offloads when predicted speedup >= threshold.
- E2E: small project compile uses local, large project offloads (mock timings).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hook honors both thresholds with deterministic behavior.
- Verbose output clearly states gating reason.
- No regression in non-compilation latency budget.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-2m7j.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- No history yet: allow remote but log "insufficient data" to avoid false local skips.
- History store unavailable/corrupt: fail-open without blocking hook.
- Threshold validation: clamp invalid values and surface in lint/logs.

## E2E Outline
- Small project + high min_local_time_ms -> local allowed with clear reason.
- Inject mock history with high speedup -> remote allowed; verify reason string.
- Verify gating does not exceed classification latency budget (<5ms).

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/hook.rs: gating decision uses min_local_time_ms and speedup threshold.
- rch-common/src/types.rs: any new timing structs serialize with defaults.

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1f5","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"## Overview

Provide shell completion support for bash/zsh/fish in a way that is ergonomic for users across environments. This bead focuses on *installation and distribution* of completions (docs, setup/installer integration, idempotent install locations), and complements the dynamic completion engine in `remote_compilation_helper-77c`.

## Goals

1. Ensure completion scripts can be installed in common shell locations
2. Provide clear docs and setup guidance for enabling completions
3. Idempotent install (no duplicate entries in rc files)
4. Work in offline or restricted environments where dynamic completions are undesirable

## Scope

- Use `rch completions <shell>` output (from 77c) as the source
- Install to standard locations:
  - bash: `~/.bash_completion.d/` or `/etc/bash_completion.d/`
  - zsh: `~/.zfunc/` + `fpath`
  - fish: `~/.config/fish/completions/`
- Optionally integrate into `rch setup` or `install.sh --easy-mode`

## Tests

- Unit: completion generation does not error
- Integration: install script writes to target location and is idempotent
- E2E: `scripts/e2e_test.sh` installs completions in temp dirs, verifies files exist and rc modifications are not duplicated

## Logging

- E2E logs should include completion output size, install path, and whether rc file was modified

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completions can be installed with clear instructions
- Idempotent install verified across shells
- Works without requiring dynamic completion mode

## Dependencies

- Dynamic completions engine (remote_compilation_helper-77c)

```
rch/tests/

    └── (linked to main e2e tests)

rchd/tests/
├── common/
│   └── mod.rs
├── integration/
│   ├── api_tests.rs     # API endpoint tests
│   └── worker_tests.rs  # Worker management tests
└── e2e/
    └── (linked to main e2e tests)
```

## Logging Setup (common/logging.rs)
```rust
use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, EnvFilter};

pub fn init_test_logging() {
    let _ = fmt()
        .with_test_writer()
        .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::from_default_env()
            .add_directive("rch=debug".parse().unwrap()))
        .try_init();
}

#[macro_export]
macro_rules! test_log {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        tracing::info!(target: "test", $($arg)*)
    };
}
```

## Fixture System (common/fixtures.rs)
```rust

    pub dir: TempDir,
    pub cargo_toml: PathBuf,
    pub src_main: PathBuf,
}

impl TestProject {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        test_log!("FIXTURE: Creating test Rust project");
        // Create minimal project
    }
}
```

- [ ] tests/ directory structure created
- [ ] Shared utilities implemented
- [ ] Logging setup works
- [ ] Fixtures reusable across tests
- [ ] cargo test discovers all tests
